basis: CIF NWE / Dated Brent physical proxy
note: Brent has eased to about $89 (WTI near $82) after touching $91.42 on Monday, its highest since 11 June — a retreat on hopes of another U.S.–Iran ceasefire, not restored supply; U.S. strikes have run a tenth consecutive night and the interim 17 June truce has broken down in practice. Physical NWE differentials remain firm with the benchmark, and refined-product tightness is acute — European low-sulphur gasoil hit a record premium near $60/bbl over Brent after Russia's export ban cut diesel loadings to about 234,000 b/d in early July. The Red Sea threat is no longer hypothetical: Yemen's Houthis have declared an embargo on all ships calling at Saudi ports (Bloomberg), directly threatening the Yanbu bypass while Hormuz runs at a near-halt (about four commodity crossings Monday, most dark). Editorial proxy, not a traded quote — indicative, and watch tanker-transit data over the rhetoric.
